nodes.title: Forward external abort signal to internal controller
nodes.why: If callers cancel the request, this utility should stop immediately too.
nodes.alternative: Ignore external signal and only support timeout-based abort.
nodes.tradeoff: More robust and flexible, but adds listener setup/cleanup work.

nodes.title: Abort automatically when timeout is reached
nodes.why: Timeout prevents requests from hanging forever and gives predictable failure behavior.
nodes.alternative: Leave timeout handling to caller code.
nodes.tradeoff: Built-in timeout is convenient, but one more path to test.

nodes.title: Throw on non-OK HTTP responses
nodes.why: Callers usually expect this helper to fail fast on HTTP errors, not silently return bad responses.
nodes.alternative: Always return response JSON and let caller inspect status.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner call sites, but less flexibility for custom status handling.

nodes.title: Always clear timer and listeners in finally
nodes.why: Cleanup in `finally` runs on success and failure, preventing leaks and stale handlers.
nodes.alternative: Duplicate cleanup in each resolve/reject branch.
nodes.tradeoff: Centralized cleanup is safer, though it requires careful variable scope.
